business.headline: India slips to 6th-largest economy: What IMF's latest data showsIndia has slipped out of the world's top five economies in nominal GDP terms, going by the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook estimates. IMF warns Asia may face sharper hit from Middle East energy shockAsia relies heavily on imported oil and gas, particularly from the Middle East. This makes it more vulnerable when supply disruptions or price spikes occur.

NPS, EPF or mutual funds: Where should you invest Rs 5,000 for 10 yearsYou set aside Rs 5,000 every month with one simple goal, i.e., to build something meaningful over 10 years. But the real question is where to invest it. NPS, EPF or mutual funds? Let's have a closer look.

Sensex slips 123 points, Nifty ends below 24,200 as markets erase early gainsThe BSE Sensex closed at 77,988.68, down 122.56 points or 0.16%. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,196.75, lower by 34.55 points or 0.14%.

DA hike has been delayed, but will you get it this year?If you've been waiting for the DA hike announcement, you might have noticed the silence this time. It hasn't come when expected, but does that mean trouble, or just a delay?

Workforce shrinks, pay hikes normalise: How TCS is reshaping hiring amid AI boomTCS has restored its salary cycle, but with more selective hiring and differentiated pay, signalling a shift towards skills and performance as the key drivers of workforce growth.

Mark Mobius dies at 89: The investor who took global money to emerging marketsMark Mobius built his reputation by investing in emerging markets that many investors once avoided due to political risk, weak governance and currency volatility. His approach helped draw billions of dollars into these regions.

8th Pay Commission: Will Rs 69,000 minimum pay actually happen?The proposal, submitted by the staff side of the National Council–Joint Consultative Machinery, has quickly grabbed attention for the scale of the increase it seeks.

Gaurav Gera: Aditya Dhar handles pressure with ease, has a no-chaos work style Gaurav Gera praised Dhurandhar director Aditya Dhar's calm and composed nature while working on the blockbuster film. He shared how Dhar's evolved mindset and easygoing approach on set left a lasting impression on him.

general news.headline: Women's Reservation Act comes into force even as House debates its implementationAn official cited 'technicalities' for bringing the law into force without elaborating. Though the act has come into force, the reservation cannot be implemented in the current House, the official said.

Live: Why debate when govt notified women's reservation act, asks DMK MPSpecial Parliament Session Live Updates: High-voltage debate on women's reservation and delimitation on Thursday saw PM Modi pitch the quota as a long-overdue democratic reform with assurances of fairness, while Amit Shah detailed the 850-seat framework and insisted no state will lose representation despite a shift in proportional share. On the other side, Priyanka Gandhi backed 33% reservation but slammed its linkage with delimitation, questioning intent, timing, and demanding immediate implementation with sub-quotas, as the Opposition warned of political motives and alleged regional imbalance. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

Calm, witty and razor-sharp - Priyanka Gandhi pulled no punches in ParliamentWithout resorting to bitterness or name-calling, Priyanka Gandhi, while speaking on delimitation, delivered barbs with a smile -- drawing laughter from Home Minister Amit Shah, energising Opposition benches, and earning praise from Rahul Gandhi.

Live: Lebanon accuses Israel of ceasefire violations, calls it acts of aggressionUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: A 10-day truce came into effect in Lebanon on Friday, aimed at pausing fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and supporting efforts to extend a broader ceasefire involving Iran, US and Israel after weeks of intense conflict. However, Lebanon's army said Israel carried out "acts of aggression" that it described as violations of the ceasefire, which took effect at midnight local time. However, the situation on the ground remains uncertain, with Hezbollah indicating that its participation in any truce depends on Israel halting all forms of hostilities. Meanwhile, the humanitarian toll continues to rise, with Lebanon's National News Agency reporting 2,196 deaths and 7,185 injuries from ongoing Israeli attacks. Additionally, US President Donald Trump said a deal to end the war with Iran was "very close" and suggested talks could resume in Islamabad as early as this weekend. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.
